title: raddia sampaiana
intro: Raddia sampaiana, also known as "Tatu-bola", is a type of plant native to the Brazilian savannas, belonging to the family Urticaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Raddia sampaiana include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used to make a topical cream or ointment for external use.
